title: Living in Boulder Vlog 5 - 21 - 2018: Google Earthing Boulder Country Roads still

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares a late-night epiphany about his Google Earth project visualizing Boulder County: instead of overlaying a color block or single image to hide modern development, he'll use screen-captured patches of natural terrain (trees, plains) to paste directly over neighborhoods, making the county appear as it did in its pre-development natural state. He also mentions being busy helping his mom organize her garage and estimates the patching work could take about a week.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited about the patch-over approach as a breakthrough solution he can't believe took him so long to figure out
Willing to throw a full week at the patching work if it produces the immersive natural landscape effect he envisions
Notes Google fuzzes out certain locations with interest but doesn't endorse their approach — his method will look more seamless

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had an epiphany since his last video about how to visually explain Boulder County by blocking out non-interesting stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is trying to recreate Boulder County in its natural state before roads and houses to superimpose an ancient realm on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to do the same visualization technique with Italy as he is doing with Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In his last video, he considered covering Boulder County with a big image or a colored polygon but found neither idea satisfactory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He realized he could patch over houses in Boulder County by using screen captures of natural landscapes to cover them.

claims.subject: Google
claims.claim: Google patches over certain areas like Babylon, making them appear as rocks.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: Google fuzzed out an area thought to be Atlantis off the southwestern coast.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to patch over neighborhoods in Boulder County by pasting screen captures of natural areas over houses.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is excited to see how Boulder County will look once he has patched over all the houses to resemble its state 100 years ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He envisions flying over Boulder County and seeing only the natural landscape with select historic towns and roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has been busy helping his mom organize her garage, limiting his time to work on the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He estimates it might take a week to patch over all the houses and roads in Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hopes the project will allow him to visually time-travel between ancient Rome and Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to upload another video in about a week once he makes progress on patching over the houses.
